sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 0.3g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a vegetable, clean nutrient density is the real test.
sections.subtitleText: 0.3g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a vegetable, clean nutrient density is the real test.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 3.3g of carbs. glycemic index, fibre, and sugar are doing most of the work. for vegetables, low downside is useful, but there still needs to be real payoff.
sections.subtitleText: 3.3g of carbs. glycemic index, fibre, and sugar are doing most of the work. for vegetables, low downside is useful, but there still needs to be real payoff.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 3.1g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is not the main pitch here, but extra support still matters.
sections.subtitleText: 3.1g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is not the main pitch here, but extra support still matters.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: minerals are basically not part of the case here.
sections.subtitleText: minerals are basically not part of the case here.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first: very low downside whole food because vegetables win a lot simply by offering clean nutritional payoff. helps add volume without much calorie cost because that makes vegetables easier to use often. good volume for the calories because that matters for satiety and general usefulness.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: on the weaker side: usually needs pairing to make a full meal because most vegetables are not complete by themselves. freshness and prep can matter because practical friction is still a real downside. taste and texture can limit compliance because a strong paper profile still needs real-world repeatability.

closing.summary: Mushrooms does have a few real-world upsides. but the nutrition case is still weak and the downsides drag it down hard. best only when you need a low-commitment add-on rather than a nutrient-dense anchor.
closing.useCaseNote: Best only when you need a low-commitment add-on rather than a nutrient-dense anchor.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Carbs are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in C tier.
